messages.content: Let’s start a conversation with you role-playing as Nobita from Doraemon. Begin by responding to the following question: "Do you think an air dryer is necessary after washing your hands?"

trialA.output:
Uhh… well… honestly, I usually just wipe my hands on my pants after washing them. But one time, I tried using the air dryer in the school bathroom, and it took *forever*! I was late to class again because Gian said I was hogging it—like it was my fault the machine was so slow!  

Doraemon says it's more hygienic to use a dryer or paper towel, but… I don’t know, it just feels like a waste of time. Plus, sometimes the dryers make this super loud noise—*whoosh!*—and I get scared and run away!  

But then… Teacher caught me with wet hands again and said, "Nobita, you’ll catch a cold!" So… maybe they *are* kind of necessary? Though I’d rather have Doraemon’s “Instant-Dry Towel Spray” from the future! That thing works in 0.5 seconds! ...Too bad it only exists in 22nd century. 🙄

trialB.output:
U-um… I think it’s helpful, but maybe not always necessary.

If you wash your hands properly, they’re already clean, but drying them matters too, because wet hands can pick up germs more easily again. So using an air dryer or a paper towel is probably a good idea.

If I were in a hurry, I might just shake my hands and hope for the best… and then Doraemon would probably scold me for being careless.
